# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM --platform=linux/amd64 ubuntu:latest

# Set working directory to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy Dockerfile from current directory
COPY . .

# Install required packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y docker.io libseccomp2 && \
    usermod -aG docker $USER

# Expose the port for Docker socket
ENV DOCKER_HOST=tcp://localhost:2375
ENV DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY=1
ENV DOCKER_CERT_PATH=/certs
ENV DOCKER_API_VERSION=1.24

# Run command to start Docker daemon in privileged mode
CMD ["dockerd", "--host=unix:///var/run/docker.sock", "--tlsverify=true", "--tlscacert=/certs/ca.pem", "--tlscert=/certs/server-cert.pem", "--tlskey=/certs/server-key.pem"]
